subject: Biology
title: The Future of Microbiology
description: A 60-second lesson on The Future of Microbiology.

hook.text: Scientists have cataloged only a fraction of Earth's microbial species. Most remain unknown.

buildup.text: Metagenomics lets researchers sequence DNA from entire ecosystems without culturing anything.

discovery.text: New antibiotics are being found in unexpected places—soil, deep ocean vents, even insect guts.

twist.text: Synthetic biology could engineer microbes to eat plastic, produce fuel, or detect cancer.

climax.text: The microbial frontier is vast. Understanding it could solve problems from disease to climate.

punchline.text: The smallest organisms hold the biggest answers.

quiz.question: What does metagenomics allow scientists to do?
options: Sequence DNA from entire ecosystems at once
Grow any microbe in a lab
